2025/12/21 20:53                                                                                 StratCom | NATO Strategic Communications Centre of Excellence Riga, Latvia

8th June 2021

China’s Social Credit System: Current Status, Role of Data and Surveillance, and
        Influence Outside of China
        By: Gulizar Haciyakupoglu

Related topics: China Social Media

Publication details
        Year: 2021

Citation

This report is an executive summary of a comprehensive, four-chapter working paper prepared by Dr Gulizar Haciyakupoglu on the Chinese Social Credit System (SCS)
          published for internal stakeholders. The working paper had explored three questions: (1) how far is the SCS from being a smoothly operating, holistic, and complete
          system; (2) what is the role of data and surveillance practices in the operation of the SCS; and (3) what influence might the SCS have outside of China. The deliberation
          of the three questions was a culmination of literature review on the SCS and interviews with 10 experts who have explored the SCS from various dimensions. Dr
          Gulizar Haciyakupoglu is a Research Fellow at the Centre of Excellence for National Security (CENS), S. Rajaratnam School of International Studies (RSIS), Nanyang
          Technological University (NTU), Singapore.

Acknowledgements: Dr Haciyakupoglu would like to thank Shang-su Wu for collaborating with her on a conference paper on the SCS and for the discussions they had
          on the topic, both of which improved her understanding of, and informed her thinking on the SCS. She also would like to acknowledge the help of Benjamin Ang, Yi-Ling
          Teo, and Muhammad Faizal Bin Abdul Rahman’s comments on the earlier versions of the working paper in shaping this work. Further, she would like to thank Alvin Lim,
          Rueban Manokara and Monika Hanley for their time and effort. Last, she would like to deliver her heartfelt appreciation and gratitude to all of the interviewees for
          sparing the time from their busy schedules to share their knowledge with her.
